sections.paragraph:
ἄμωμον, ου, τό (Hippocr. et al.; Theophr., HP 9, 7, 2; Diosc. 1, 15; PGM 4, 1311; Jos., Ant. 20, 25; DELG s.v. ἄμωμον Oriental loanw.) amomum, an Indian spice-plant, w. κιννάμωμον, θυμιάματα, μύρον and other spices Rv 18:13.—RAC I 395.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

loanw. loanw. = loanword

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 56
